title: Yang-Mills Convergence — Gauge Fields on the Lattice
author: forgemaster

content.theorem: Yang-Mills field configurations on a discrete lattice converge to continuum solutions as the lattice spacing a → 0, with corrections of O(a²) for improved actions
content.proof: Wilson's lattice gauge theory: gauge fields live on links as group elements U_μ(x) ∈ SU(N). The Yang-Mills action S = β Σ (1 - 1/N Re Tr U_{plaq}) where U_{plaq} is the product of link variables around a plaquette. As a → 0, S → continuum Yang-Mills action with O(a²) corrections. Improved actions (Symanzik, Iwasaki) reduce corrections to O(a⁴).
content.benchmark: No real benchmark. Theoretical projections: SU(2) on 16⁴ lattice: ~10⁶ updates/sec on GPU. SU(3) on 32⁴ lattice: ~10⁵ updates/sec. Convergence: ~10⁴ sweeps for decorrelation.
content.caveat: Highly theoretical. The Yang-Mills mass gap (Clay Millennium Problem) is unsolved. We study the LATTICE version, not the continuum problem. The connection to constraint theory is through holonomy.
